programme.title: Yudra
programme.desc: Seorang pemuda yang diliputi hasrat balas dendam, Yudhra menyusup ke dalam kartel yang kejam untuk membalas dendam atas pembunuhan orang tuanya, namun justru menemukan kebenaran kelam tentang masa lalunya yang akan menguji batas kemampuannya.

programme.title: Classic Ride
programme.desc: It is the "Holy Grail" for Porsche fans and the first Porsche to ever bear the famous name of the design genius from Austria: The Type 64, a racing car from 1938 that is based on the Volkswagen developed by Professor Ferdinand Porsche. Only two of the streamlined cars were built and were intended to take part in a race from Berlin to Rome. But the war intervened... (channel info).

programme.title: Cars + Life
programme.desc: After more than 70 years, Land Rover presents a new edition of the classic Defender off-road vehicle. Also: the world record attempt in drifting in the Porsche Taycan at the Hockenheimring. And: the Porsche 911 Turbo and the 718 Spyder in the test. (Station info).

programme.title: Car History
programme.desc: The 60s and 70s – the golden years for Opel. The traditional brand is at its best. Ascona, Manta, Rekord and GT: Opel is more than just a car, Opel is an attitude to life. Opel is colorful, fashionable, exciting - and builds one classic after another. Today they are all cult. Car History shows the heyday of Opel. (Station info).

programme.title: Nordschleife
programme.desc: And only here does the largest motorsport spectacle in the world take place. 210 racing cars, more than 1,000 drivers and helpers, plus over 200,000 fans. The 24 hour race leaves no one indifferent. (Station info).

programme.title: On Tour
programme.desc: What is the correct name? The boat, the boat or the boat? Well, today it's the Boot for once, because we're making a little trip to Düsseldorf with Motorvision on Tour and that's where the Boot is taking place and that's the world's largest yacht and water sports trade fair in the entire world. (Station info).

programme.title: Perfect Ride
programme.desc: Size matters! At least many families will agree. With the BMW XM, M GmbH is presenting itself with a true colossus for families with certain demands for its 50th birthday. The fully electric KIA EV9 is significantly quieter, but visually similarly brutal. In the family check we find out whether it is perhaps the best electric car for large families. (Station info).

programme.title: Crash
programme.desc: When ambition exceeds driving ability and when well-secured racing professionals are carried out of the curve, then we are there with the camera. Breathtaking stunts of professional drivers and slapsticks of sophisticated tin bullets. Spectacular scenes in which no one is harmed. (Station info).

programme.title: A&E
programme.desc: The West Midlands Ambulance Service is called to millions of emergencies every year. The operations are often so intense that treatment begins on the street. The team of dedicated doctors and paramedics has the daily task of saving lives. (Station info).

programme.title: Motorcycles
programme.desc: Three scooters, one idea: It doesn't always have to be the classic Vespa with a combustion engine. Alternative 1: The e-max electric scooter made in China. Alternative 2: The Taiwanese large scooter Kymco xciting 500i with 38 HP and strengths in the city as well as on tours. Alternative 3: The Piaggio MP3 three-wheel scooter, which you can drive with a car license because of its wide front axle. Which is the most exciting alternative to the Vespa? (Station info).

programme.title: Mission Mobility
programme.desc: Dubai - city of luxury, fast cars and incredible stories. Just like a man's: his passion is cars: fast cars. Nice cars. Special cars. And he has two dream jobs: He is a racing driver and president of the Porsche Club in the United Arab Emirates. Karim Al Azhari loves and lives luxury, but every now and then he is really stressed. For example, when, like now, he has to organize a motorsport event on the Abu Dhabi racing circuit. (Station info).

programme.title: Isle of Man Tourist Trophy
programme.desc: The Isle of Man Tourist Trophy is considered the toughest and most dangerous motorcycle race in the world, with riders reaching speeds of up to 200mph. (Station info).

programme.title: Made in....
programme.desc: Mercedes Benz celebrates 125 years of automotive history. The brand name only emerged in 1926 from the merger of the brands Mercedes, Daimler-Motoren-Gesellschaft, and Benz. In addition to great quality, Mercedes-Benz vehicles also stand for safety. The Mercedes star is the most important identifying mark of Mercedes-Benz cars. The name “Mercedes-Benz” is considered the epitome of tradition and innovation for the future of the automobile. (Station info).

programme.title: Dream Cars
programme.desc: Ferrari. The sports cars with the Prancing Horse in the coat of arms are the epitome of elegant and hot-blooded appearance. No other sports car contains as much magic and myth as the fiery red icons from Maranello. Whether on the road or on the racetrack, where the cars with the Prancing Horse on their crest start, they are the center of attention - Ferrari has that certain something that other brands have been looking for for 50 years. Whether LaFerrari, 488Spyder or the legend F40, Tetsa Rossa or 250 GTO, whether Formula 1 or the Ferrari World in Dubai, whether Sebastian Vettel or Niki Lauda, ​​Dream Cars shows the most exciting cars in red, takes a look (channel information).

programme.title: Crash
programme.desc: When ambition exceeds driving ability and when well-secured racing professionals are carried out of the curve, then we are there with the camera. Breathtaking stunts of professional drivers and slapsticks of sophisticated tin bullets. Spectacular scenes in which no one is harmed. (Station info).

programme.title: Classic Ride
programme.desc: They offer driving fun with a guarantee: classic convertibles with a lot of tinder. This species includes, for example, the Sunbeam Tiger. Under the hood of the compact roadster is a powerful V8 engine from Ford USA. With a heavy foot on the accelerator, the Tiger driver easily draws 20-meter-long lines on the asphalt. Today the Sunbeam Tiger is a cheap alternative to an incredibly expensive Shelby Cobra. Also in the show: A Beetle convertible from 1951 with Porsche power. (Station info).

programme.title: Cars + Life
programme.desc: In addition to environmental pollution and the climate crisis, increasing traffic volumes are also encouraging cities to develop concepts for modern traffic management. We will shed light on the situation using Hamburg as an example. Also: the winter test of the Porsche Taygan. And: the Volkswagen ID.4 and the new Mercedes-Benz S-Class in the driving report. (Station info).

programme.title: Car History
programme.desc: There is a car in the universe that everyone knows and that almost everyone has ridden in. A car that was given a nickname and hasn't been called anything else since. A car that has conquered every continent: the VW Beetle. Built over 22 million times and over 60 years old, it tells more about our century than many history books. Car History tells the story of the indestructible Beetle, from the first 24.5 HP model to the Rally Beetle, the Mexico Beetle and the latest model of the New Beetle. (Station info).

programme.title: Nordschleife
programme.desc: Racing legend Jackie Stewart once described the Nordschleife of the Nürburgring as a multi-limbed monster and gave it the name 'Green Hell'. The legendary track is also regularly accessible to the general public. Many “racers at heart” race through Schwedenkreuz, Fuchsrohre, Kesselchen and Karussell in their private cars. Once through the “Green Hell” a dream for many that no one has to stay. Nordschleife fans take the spectators on board and tell them what they associate with the ring. (Station info).

programme.title: On Tour
programme.desc: Dubai – the largest and most important Motor Sow in the Middle East takes place here. The sheikhs have a penchant for fast and exclusive vehicles, so everything that has a reputation in the car world is gathered here. And: Supercars go straight from the stand in Dubai. (Station info).

programme.title: Perfect Ride
programme.desc: The future of mobility will be diverse! Also for people who want to drive themselves. BMW presents mixed reality: driving virtual routes in a real car. This is already possible today with the BMW Driving Experience. The Mercedes Vision AVTR is still a long way from being ready for series production. The concept car is inspired by the film Avatar and is intended to literally merge with the driver. (Station info).

programme.title: Rear view mirror
programme.desc: The Jaguar E-Type appears in the rearview mirror today, a car for winners. It was built from 1961 to 1975 and was sold with great success, especially in the USA, due to its exciting design. (Station info).

programme.title: Tuning – it doesn’t get any lower than that!
programme.desc: GT used to stand for Gran Tourisme - high-powered sports cars for endurance racing. Today the FIA ​​regulates various GT classes in motorsport. For the manufacturers, the GTs are the icing on the cake of the model series. And because that's not enough, there are S versions of the infamous GT vehicles. Mercedes calls the GT that and goes one better with the GT S. Porsche can also do GTS and J-Sport has taken the Subaru GT 86 and converted it into a real performance slingshot. GT also stands for big dreams in this episode. There are three of them and in very different performance classes. (Station info).

programme.title: Stunt Heroes
programme.desc: The Stunt Heroes want to jump with a car straight through a new ambulance. This episode of the action series shows how this works and how to implement such a stunt. (Station info).

programme.title: Top Speed Classic
programme.desc: Jochen Mass would have been 79 years old on September 30th. Unfortunately, the former Formula 1 driver, who died in May, was no longer able to experience that. In this episode, Top Speed ​​presenter Martin Utberg and Ralf Eisenbach bring back memories - in the Agnesenhof, the hotel where Jochen always stayed when he was on the Nürburgring or Nordschleife. (Station info).

programme.title: Super Cars
programme.desc: The days of slow, boring electric cars are finally over. With the Rimac Concept One the door opens into an exciting new world. This super sports car from Croatia generates 1088 hp and 1600 Newton meters of torque purely electrically. The resulting performance is breathtaking. It seems as if the Concept One has landed from the future to redefine the world of super sports. Driving fun straight from the socket – in supercars. (Station info).

programme.title: New Zealand Jetsprint Championship
programme.desc: The New Zealand Jetsprint Championship is the most important sprint boat competition from New Zealand, the country where the sport was founded in 1981. Jetsprinting is comparable to rally sports, but there is only one boat on the track at any given time and boats drive at very high speeds. The up to 500 HP strong jet sprint boats therefore try to achieve the best time on the tracks. (Senderinfo).

programme.title: UIM Aquabike World Championship 2025
programme.sub-title: GP of Qatar, Runabout & Freestyle, Old Doha Port

programme.title: Isle of Man Tourist Trophy
programme.desc: The Isle of Man Tourist Trophy is considered the toughest and most dangerous motorcycle race in the world, with riders reaching speeds of up to 200mph. (Station info).

programme.title: Top Speed Classic
programme.desc: Moderator Martin Utberg takes part in the Spalatin Historic, a one-day classic car rally in aid of a good cause. Together with organizer Stefan von Heyden, Martin discovers the Franconian region. During the various breaks, entertaining special stages ensure many smiling faces among participants and spectators. In this program, Top Speed ​​also takes viewers to Lucerne, where Martin gets involved behind the scenes at a classic car auction by the Oldtimer Galerie Toffen at the Swiss Classic World. (Station info).

programme.title: Car History
programme.desc: It is the second largest automobile manufacturer in the world, Volkswagen AG. It all begins in 1936 with a car, the VW Beetle. Back then, the Beetle was supposed to make the dream of a Volkswagen come true. There was hardly anything that the Beetle wasn't: a propaganda object, construction helper, million-seller, film star or sports equipment. The Beetle and the Transporter made VW world famous, and the factory in Wolfsburg grew into a multinational company. The Beetle becomes the best-selling car in the world. But VW is building a modern successor almost too late and is heading into crisis. (Station info).

programme.title: Top Speed Classic
programme.desc: Moderator Martin Utberg and his team partner Günter Baier are taking part in the Franken Classic, one of the most beautiful vintage car rallies in Germany. The participants discover Franconia and its most beautiful sides on picturesque routes. (Station info).

programme.title: Rear view mirror
programme.desc: Super Seven – when you hear these words, car fans first think of Lotus. Because Colin Chapman developed the open kit racer, i.e. a kit car, in the 1950s. (Station info).

programme.title: Racing Files
programme.sub-title: McLaren – from Formula 1 to Can Am
programme.desc: McLaren is the second oldest active team in Formula 1 and now enjoys worldwide fame. However, when it was founded, hardly anyone would have expected that the team founded by New Zealand racing driver Bruce McLaren would develop into a heavyweight in the world of super sports cars and one of the best-known teams in motorsport. Racing Files tells the story of McLaren. (Station info).

programme.desc: The Isle of Man Tourist Trophy is considered the toughest and most dangerous motorcycle race in the world, with riders reaching speeds of up to 200mph. (Station info).

programme.desc: The West Midlands Ambulance Service is called to millions of emergencies every year. The operations are often so intense that treatment begins on the street. The team of dedicated doctors and paramedics has the daily task of saving lives. (Station info).

programme.title: Cars + Life
programme.desc: On the occasion of Skoda's 125th anniversary, we look back at the eventful history of one of the oldest automobile manufacturers in the world. Also: our practical test of the Volkswagen ID.3, the first purely electric car from VW. And: the McLaren 720S Spider, the super sports car is now also available as a convertible. (Station info).

programme.title: Motorcycles
programme.desc: “BMW Motorrad Vision Next 100” is the name of the motorcycle of the future. A helmet is no longer necessary, connectivity delivers all the information on a pair of driver glasses. In addition to the world premiere of the Future Bike in Santa Monica, our Los Angeles special takes us into the present with the new BMW G 310 R and into the past on the L.A. road trip: Designer Ola Stenegard draws inspiration from bygone eras. (Station info).

programme.title: Mission Mobility
programme.desc: A container on a gravel lot, surrounded by a colorful mix of used cars, some still in good condition, others a little polished, the previous owner has already left his fingerprint here. What is behind these slightly cranky-looking car dealers? In Motorvision TV, people in the automotive profession tell their stories. (Station info).

programme.title: Isle of Man Tourist Trophy
programme.desc: The Isle of Man Tourist Trophy is considered the toughest and most dangerous motorcycle race in the world, with riders reaching speeds of up to 200mph. (Station info).

programme.title: Made in....
programme.desc: A new chapter in automotive history begins at the end of 2001. From now on, Volkswagen wants to be involved in the luxury class. The birth of the VW Phaeton. He gets his own glass factory. A 55,000 square meter production facility is being built in the middle of Dresden for around 187 million euros, where the Wolfsburg-based company's top model will be handcrafted. (Station info).

programme.title: Green wave
programme.desc: The Zero Emission Rally 2010 – here the best emission-free vehicles demonstrate their performance and roadworthiness. For three days, the participants compete in a 750-kilometer race through Norway and produce hardly any CO2 emissions! (Station info).

programme.title: Dream Cars
programme.desc: Whether Mercedes S-Class, BMW 7 Series or Audi A8, German sedans enjoy a worldwide reputation. They are famous for their perfection and prestige. Limousines Made in Germany are the epitome of status and they set the standards in terms of comfort, pioneering technology and safety systems. DreamCars presents the most famous, beautiful and luxurious German limousines. (Station info).

programme.title: Crash
programme.desc: When ambition exceeds driving ability and when well-secured racing professionals are carried out of the curve, then we are there with the camera. Breathtaking stunts of professional drivers and slapsticks of sophisticated tin bullets. Spectacular scenes in which no one is harmed. (Station info).

programme.title: Classic Ride
programme.desc: For years they were laughed at and despised in the classic car scene. Classic automobiles from Japan. But since a Toyota 2000 GT from 1967 was sold at an auction in the USA for 1.2 million dollars, they have had a different status. Nippon oldies are no longer just considered bizarre, but have become socially acceptable. Classic Ride presents vintage and classic cars made in Japan. (Station info).

programme.title: Cars + Life
programme.desc: The new mobile magazine presents the latest trends and innovations in the automotive industry every week. Whether compact everyday heroes, windy city runabouts for the urban jungle, beefy SUVs for family trips or modern electric and hydrogen vehicles: our experts will take you on a test drive through the world of automobility of today and tomorrow. (Station info).

programme.title: Car History
programme.desc: Nobody is faster, nobody is more reliable, nobody is more successful. Whether Le Mans, the Monte Carlo Rally or Formula 1: There is no race or rally that Porsche has not won, 28,000 times in 65 years. World record. Porsche wins a race with the first prototype, unforgettable the successes of the James Dean sypder at the Carrera Panamericana or the Targa Florio. In 1970 the first Le Mans victory with the 'monster', the Porsche 917. (Station info).

programme.title: Nordschleife
programme.desc: Racing legend Jackie Stewart once described the Nordschleife of the Nürburgring as a multi-limbed monster and gave it the name “Green Hell”. The legendary track is also regularly accessible to the general public. Many “racers at heart” race through Schwedenkreuz, Fuchsrohre, Kesselchen and Karussell in their private cars. Once through the “Green Hell” – a dream for many that no one has to stay. Nordschleife fans take the spectators on board and tell them what they associate with the ring. (Station info).

programme.title: On Tour
programme.desc: BMW M1, the legendary vehicles of the Procar series, are all back at the start in Salzburg for the first time. (Station info).

programme.title: Perfect Ride
programme.desc: Robust and popular - these are the keywords for these models. Subaru has renamed the XV. Completely renovated and with the new name Cros-strek, the SUV is now looking for customers. We reveal what's behind it. And Cupra is giving its bestseller Formentor an update that is particularly causing a visual sensation. We explain why. (Station info).

programme.title: Rear view mirror
programme.sub-title: Viva Mexico! The Panamericana in the 50s
programme.desc: The Mexicana Panamericana is one of the most difficult and longest road races. Mercedes used its almost unbeatable 300 SL in the 50s. The Porsche 550 Spyder is a myth. Not just because James Dean had a fatal accident in this vehicle. Hans Herrmann is considered one of the best German racing drivers ever. The victory in the Porsche 917 at Le Mans or his accident on the Avus in Berlin will never be forgotten. (Station info).

programme.title: Tuning – it doesn’t get any lower than that!
programme.desc: BMW's in-house tuner is M-GmbH. Anything that isn't in the garage at three is made really sharp in the hallowed halls of the M tuners. And not only is there more power under the hood, the chassis, brakes and the entire body also have to be on the exercise bike. Pounds down, looks up, performance in, sound out. It's not an easy workout but it's worth the effort. And then the horsepower fireworks are fired. The M4 with 431 hp, the M5 and the M6 ​​Grand Coupé with 560 hp each. The M family is a happy family. And the secret of their success is their pronounced athleticism. (Station info).

programme.title: Stunt Heroes
programme.desc: Another new stunt test from action concept's "Stunt Heroes": Stuntman Tobi, called "Heidi", will remotely control an ArtegaGT at full speed. But this is just practice for the big stunt in which he has to drive a car up a steep ramp at 110km/h. The question: how high can remote-controlled cars fly? Action spectacle with flying element! (Station info).

programme.title: Top Speed Classic
programme.sub-title: Top Speed on Tour in Steyr
programme.desc: In this episode of Top Speed ​​on Tour, Austrian classic car expert Franz Wittner takes presenter Martin Utberg along in a 1929 Steyr Type 12. Together they explore the city of Steyr, which Martin has visited many times, but was never able to stay for more than an hour because his visits always took place during the lunch breaks of the famous Ennstal Classic. This time Franz shows him the most beautiful places in this historic city. (Station info).

programme.title: Super Cars
programme.desc: British cars are considered elegant, exclusive and extremely stylish. The Bentley Continental GT Speed ​​is a coupé with enormous performance: 12 cylinders, 625 hp and 820 Newton meters. The Alps offer the right setting to really enjoy the potential of this special all-wheel drive vehicle. And: The Rapide S is a stylish Gran Coupé. Despite its four doors, it is clearly recognizable as an Aston Martin. V12 engine, 560 hp and 630 Newton meters of torque. These numbers speak for themselves. (Station info).

programme.title: Car History
programme.desc: It is the second largest automobile manufacturer in the world, Volkswagen AG. It all begins in 1936 with a car, the VW Beetle. Back then, the Beetle was supposed to make the dream of a Volkswagen come true. There was hardly anything that the Beetle wasn't: a propaganda object, construction helper, million-seller, film star or sports equipment. The Beetle and the Transporter made VW world famous, and the factory in Wolfsburg grew into a multinational company. The Beetle becomes the best-selling car in the world. But VW is building a modern successor almost too late and is heading into crisis. (Station info).

programme.title: Top Speed Classic
programme.desc: Moderator Martin Utberg and his team partner Günter Baier are taking part in the Franken Classic, one of the most beautiful vintage car rallies in Germany. The participants discover Franconia and its most beautiful sides on picturesque routes. (Station info).

programme.title: Classic Ride
programme.desc: Classic Rides rummages through the automotive treasure chest and brings to light some almost forgotten, some noble, some bizarre cars from the last few decades. For example the Amphi Cars. Whether on the road or in the water, the extraordinary vehicles are incredibly maneuverable and an absolute eye-catcher. (Station info).

programme.title: Top Speed Classic
programme.desc: Moderator Martin Utberg and his team partner Günter Baier are taking part in the Franken Classic, one of the most beautiful vintage car rallies in Germany. The participants discover Franconia and its most beautiful sides on picturesque routes. (Station info).

programme.title: Isle of Man Tourist Trophy
programme.desc: Another total cancellation of the race day on the Isle of Man due to the weather, but instead we had the opportunity to talk in more detail with the Crowe brothers and Davey Todd and take a look back at the most spectacular races of the week. (Station info).

programme.title: Icelandic Formula Off-Road
programme.desc: Formula Off Road was invented in Iceland. Hoping to raise more money for the Icelandic rescue teams, the teams began showing their 4WD cars in action in the Icelandic hills. (Station info).

programme.title: Crash
programme.desc: When ambition exceeds driving ability and when well-secured racing professionals are carried out of the curve, then we are there with the camera. Breathtaking stunts of professional drivers and slapsticks of sophisticated tin bullets. Spectacular scenes in which no one is harmed. (Station info).

programme.title: Isle of Man Tourist Trophy 2026
programme.desc: Another total cancellation of the race day on the Isle of Man due to the weather, but instead we had the opportunity to talk in more detail with the Crowe brothers and Davey Todd and take a look back at the most spectacular races of the week. (Station info).

programme.title: A&E
programme.desc: The West Midlands Ambulance Service is called to millions of emergencies every year. The operations are often so intense that treatment begins on the street. The team of dedicated doctors and paramedics has the daily task of saving lives. (Station info).

programme.title: Cars + Life
programme.desc: Off-road vehicle fans can't ignore the Jeep brand. We accompanied a family from Spain at Camp Jeep in Lyon. Also: the Porsche Taycan in the summer test. And: Audi's crossover SUV Q3 Sportback Plug-In Hybrid and the fourth generation Kia Sorento in the driving report. (Station info).

programme.title: Classic Ride
programme.desc: For years they were laughed at and despised in the classic car scene. Classic automobiles from Japan. But since a Toyota 2000 GT from 1967 was sold at an auction in the USA for 1.2 million dollars, they have had a different status. Nippon oldies are no longer just considered bizarre, but have become socially acceptable. Classic Ride presents vintage and classic cars made in Japan. (Station info).

programme.title: Cars + Life
programme.desc: The new mobile magazine presents the latest trends and innovations in the automotive industry every week. Whether compact everyday heroes, windy city runabouts for the urban jungle, beefy SUVs for family trips or modern electric and hydrogen vehicles: our experts will take you on a test drive through the world of automobility of today and tomorrow. (Station info).

programme.title: Car History
programme.desc: Nobody is faster, nobody is more reliable, nobody is more successful. Whether Le Mans, the Monte Carlo Rally or Formula 1: There is no race or rally that Porsche has not won, 28,000 times in 65 years. World record. Porsche wins a race with the first prototype, unforgettable the successes of the James Dean sypder at the Carrera Panamericana or the Targa Florio. In 1970 the first Le Mans victory with the 'monster', the Porsche 917. (Station info).

programme.title: Nordschleife
programme.desc: Racing legend Jackie Stewart once described the Nordschleife of the Nürburgring as a multi-limbed monster and gave it the name “Green Hell”. The legendary track is also regularly accessible to the general public. Many “racers at heart” race through Schwedenkreuz, Fuchsrohre, Kesselchen and Karussell in their private cars. Once through the “Green Hell” – a dream for many that no one has to stay. Nordschleife fans take the spectators on board and tell them what they associate with the ring. (Station info).

programme.title: On Tour
programme.desc: BMW M1, the legendary vehicles of the Procar series, are all back at the start in Salzburg for the first time. (Station info).

programme.title: Perfect Ride
programme.desc: Robust and popular - these are the keywords for these models. Subaru has renamed the XV. Completely renovated and with the new name Cros-strek, the SUV is now looking for customers. We reveal what's behind it. And Cupra is giving its bestseller Formentor an update that is particularly causing a visual sensation. We explain why. (Station info).

programme.title: Crash
programme.desc: When ambition exceeds driving ability and when well-secured racing professionals are carried out of the curve, then we are there with the camera. Breathtaking stunts of professional drivers and slapsticks of sophisticated tin bullets. Spectacular scenes in which no one is harmed. (Station info).

programme.title: Going Pro 2026
programme.desc: The seventh season of the Going Pro TV series follows Damon Surzyshyn and the entire PROsport Racing team in the 2026 ADAC GT4 Germany season (channel info).

programme.title: Fastzone 2026
programme.desc: The show takes the audience into the fascinating world of international racing. The format combines thrilling motorsport action with in-depth analysis and provides a comprehensive overview of important racing series around the globe. In addition to spectacular scenes on the track, the format offers exciting background reports, technical insights and stories about drivers, teams and their challenges. This creates a diverse look at speed, competition and the passion that drives motorsport worldwide. (Station info).

programme.title: Biker Lifestyle
programme.desc: The north of Thailand is a popular travel destination, not just for adventurous individual tourists. The north is known for its mountainous, densely forested areas and therefore this region is also a paradise for motorcyclists. Thousands of curves on well-developed roads with little traffic and a diversity of culture and landscape make this region a perfect holiday destination for us to escape the winter in Germany for the fourth time. We will take you on a 1600 km long motorcycle tour to the most beautiful places in northern Thailand. (Station info).

Five exciting facts and a bonus about the Hyundai Ioniq 5 N. (channel information) await you.

programme.title: New Zealand Jetsprint Championship
programme.desc: The New Zealand Jetsprint Championship is the most important sprint boat competition from New Zealand, the country where the sport was founded in 1981. Jetsprinting is comparable to rally sports, but there is only one boat on the track at any given time and boats drive at very high speeds. The up to 500 HP strong jet sprint boats therefore try to achieve the best time on the tracks. (Senderinfo).

programme.title: FIM X-Trial World Championship 2026
programme.desc: The Fédération Internationale de Motocyclisme has organized the X-Trial World Championship annually since 1993. In contrast to the outdoor discipline, the X-Trail competitions take place indoors. (Station info).

programme.title: Belgian Rally Championship 2026
programme.desc: The Belgian Rally Championship is one of the most competitive rally championships at national level. This also attracts prominent guest starters such as Thierry Neuville, Craig Breen and Stéphane Lefebvre. (Station info).

programme.title: World Rally Raid Championship 2025
programme.desc: The international rally event has been part of the “FIA Marathon Rally Worldcup” since 2017. Previously it was the closing event of the “Middle East Rally Championship”. The record winner is Mohammed bin Sulayem. (Station info).

programme.desc: Super Seven – when you hear these words, car fans first think of Lotus. Because Colin Chapman developed the open kit racer, i.e. a kit car, in the 1950s. (Station info).

programme.title: Racing Files
programme.sub-title: McLaren – from Formula 1 to Can Am
programme.desc: McLaren is the second oldest active team in Formula 1 and now enjoys worldwide fame. However, when it was founded, hardly anyone would have expected that the team founded by New Zealand racing driver Bruce McLaren would develop into a heavyweight in the world of super sports cars and one of the best-known teams in motorsport. Racing Files tells the story of McLaren. (Station info).

programme.title: That Dirty Black Bag
programme.sub-title: Chapter One: A Head Weighs Less Than a Body
programme.desc: Far off in the West, bounty hunter Red Bill searches for the man who slew his mother; in nearby Greenvale, where it hasn't rained for years, local farmer Steve finds gold beneath his land; Sheriff McCoy comes upon a small fortune.

programme.title: That Dirty Black Bag
programme.desc: After Red Bill is captured, old memories come back to haunt him; McCoy comes upon Butler's farm; Steve and Eve's relationship woes intensify.

programme.title: That Dirty Black Bag
programme.desc: Red Bill travels the barren wastes with McCoy and Kurt; in Greenvale, Eve's struggle to maintain her business is threatened; Thompson's claim to Steve's land is taken up a notch.

programme.title: That Dirty Black Bag
programme.desc: Fifteen years earlier, Red Bill learns about being a man from a rugged Civil War veteran, Anderson; McCoy and his vile older brother, Bronson, must resort to increasingly dark means as they struggle to survive the desert.

programme.title: That Dirty Black Bag
programme.desc: Red Bill, McCoy and Kurt enter the mines, where trouble ensues; Eve faces dire consequences for her actions; a modern-day Bronson runs for governor.

programme.title: That Dirty Black Bag
programme.desc: Rain comes to Greenvale, bringing with it great struggle; Bronson's men encounter McCoy and Red Bill; unexpected reunions abound.

programme.title: That Dirty Black Bag
programme.desc: In the wake of the shoot-offs with Bronson's men, Red Bill and McCoy must come together for a common goal; Thompson's pressure on Steve reaches a boiling point.

programme.title: That Dirty Black Bag
programme.desc: Red Bill and McCoy's quest for Bronson comes to a breaking point, as impossible decisions and realizations are made; Bronson takes a new step toward power; Steve faces Thompson in a final standoff.
